questions.question: The ______ SQL keyword combination is used to return only unique values.
The ______ SQL statement may be used with aggregate functions to group the result-set by\none or more columns.
A(n) ______ on a table is typically added to speed up the performance of queries.
Which of the below SQL statements will filter to records with FruitName values that are not\nMango, Apple, or Jackfruit?
What is the purpose of the below SQL?\nINSERT INTO Categories (CategoryID, CategoryName)\nVALUES(100, 'Electronics');
options: Insert two rows into the Categories table
Create the Categories table and then insert a row into the table
Insert a row into the Categories table

questions.question: What would be the effect of the below DELETE statement, assuming CITIES is a table\ncontaining multiple records.\nDELETE FROM CITIES;
options: The statement removes the last row from the table CITIES;
The statement has no effect on the table CITIES;
The statement removes the first row from the table CITIES;
The statement removes all rows from the table CITIES;

questions.question: What would be the effect of the below DELETE statement?\nDELETE FROM Products\nWHERE ProductID = 1;
options: The statement removes any rows from the Products table that have ProductID with\nvalue 1.
The statement removes any rows from the Products table that have ProductID\nstarting with 1.
The statement removes all rows from the Products table.
The statement removes no rows from the Products even if there is a row with\nProductID = 1.

questions.question: What would be the effect of the below UPDATE statement? Assume Products is a table\ncontaining multiple records.\nUPDATE Products SET ProductName = 'My Favorite Product';
options: The statement changes only the first row to have ProductName with value My\nFavorite Product.
The statement would error, since the UPDATE statement requires a WHERE clause.
The statement changes all rows in the Products table to have ProductName with\nvalue My Favorite Product.

questions.question: The ______ statement removes all rows from a table and frees the space allocated for the\ntable records.
The acronym DCL stands for
Which of the following is an example of DCL?
A Foreign Key constraint may be created to link two columns within the same table?
The Order By keyword sorts records in ascending order by default?
3NF is typically used in the practice of ______.
A ______ is a set of SQL statements with an assigned name stored in the database so that it\nmay be reused.
A database ______ is procedural code that executes automatically in response to certain\nevents, including DML events.
In the Oracle database management system, which of the following is a procedural language\nextension to SQL?
Which of the following database systems is a RDBMS?
A ______ is a column or a set of columns in a table that uniquely identifies a row.
A ______ for a table is a unique identifier, for records, that has business meaning and is\noften derived from application data. International Standard Book Number (ISBN) would be\nan example.
Database tables can be created without a Primary Key.
With respect to a Foreign Key Constraint, the table containing the Foreign Key is the\n______ table and the table containing the Candidate Key is the Parent table.
Which of the following joins, essentially, combines the results of both left and right outer\njoins?
Which range of values defines SMALLINT?
In SQL Server and Postgres, which range of values defines BIGINT?
In Postgres, which of the below SQL will return the current date and time?
The date range for the DATE datatype in Postgres is ______.

What would be returned by the below SQL, assuming CityName and CountryName have the
same datatype?
SELECT CityName FROM Cities
UNION
SELECT CountryName FROM Countries;
options: A distinct list of CityName values from Cities that also exist as a CountryName in\nCountries
A non-distinct list of CityName values from the Cities table combined with the\nCountryName values from Countries
A distinct list of the following: CityName values from the Cities table combined with\nthe CountryName values from Countries
An error would be returned, as the column name is not the same between the two\nSelect statements

questions.question: Which of the following may be used to perform an inner join between the Customers and\nOrders tables using their CustID columns?
options: Inner Join Orders ON Customers.CustID = Orders.CustID

questions.question: Which of the below statements adds the column ClientDescription with datatype\nVARCHAR(10) to the Clients table?
options: AMMEND TABLE Clients ADD COLUMN ClientDescription VARCHAR(10);
ALTER TABLE Clients AMMEND ClientDescription VARCHAR(10);
ALTER TABLE Clients ADD ClientDescription VARCHAR(10);
MODIFY TABLE Clients ADD COLUMN ClientDescription VARCHAR(10);

questions.question: Which of the following creates a Foreign Key?
In a RDBMS, a “relation” is physically represented by a ______.
In Postgres and MySQL, which SQL statement is used to restrict the number of records\nreturned by a query result set to a specified number of rows?
With respect to Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance (CAP), relational databases\ntypically provide ______.
A ______ is sequence of database operations that satisfies the ACID principles.
Which of the following is not a date/time data type in Postgres?
______ guarantees that a transaction can only bring the database from one valid state to\nanother valid state.
Due to ______ once a transaction has completed execution, the updates to the database are\npersisted in a way that is recoverable upon system failure.
Which of the below SQL statements tests for a column having a null value?
The BETWEEN operator is inclusive. The begin and end values are included.
A table or column may be given a temporary name within a query by using a(n) ______.
What is the result of the below SQL?\nSELECT COUNT(*) FROM Orders;

How many rows are returned by the below SQL?
SELECT CustomerID, SUM(OrderTotal) AS OrderTotals
FROM Orders
GROUP BY CustomerID
HAVING SUM(OrderTotal) > 12;
Which of the below SQL statements will return a single row with the value 3, which is the\nnumber of different CustomerID values in the Orders table?
options: SELECT CustomerID from Orders GROUP BY CustomerID;

questions.question: If additional rows were inserted into the Orders table, which column could accept a NULL\nvalue?
Which of the below SQL will return the value 5.0000, which is the lowest OrderTotal value?

Which of the below SQL will return the following results set?
-----------
|CustomerID | HighestTotal|
|1 | 15.0000|
|3 | 10.0000|
|4 | 10.0000|
-----------
options: SELECT CustomerID, MAX(OrderTotal) AS HighestTotal FROM Orders GROUP\nBY CustomerID ORDER BY CustomerID;
SELECT CustomerID, CEILING(OrderTotal) AS HighestTotal FROM Orders\nGROUP BY CustomerID ORDER BY CustomerID;
SELECT CustomerID, HIGHEST(OrderTotal) AS HighestTotal FROM Orders\nGROUP BY CustomerID ORDER BY CustomerID;
SELECT CustomerID, TOP(HighestTotal) AS HighestTotal FROM Orders GROUP\nBY CustomerID ORDER BY CustomerID;

questions.question: In Postgres and SQL Server, what would the below SQL do?\nSELECT * INTO ProductsCopy\nFROM Products;
options: Creates a new table called ProductsCopy and then copies the Products table data into\nthat table.
Copies Products table data into ProductsCopy if ProductsCopy already exists.
Creates a new table called ProductsCopy and then copies the Product table data into\nthat table and then removes all rows from the Product table.
Attempts to create a new table called ProductsCopy if it doesn’t already exist and\nthen copies the Products table data into that table.

questions.question: As of SQL Server 2017 and earlier, which of the following is not a numeric data type in\nSQL Server?
The date ranges for the DATE datatype in SQL Server, Postgres, and MySQL are identical?
